bull.b: Nexters’ core free-to-play model drives recurring revenue via microtransactions and live-ops. This business model creates predictable lifetime value, recurring in-game spend, and scalability across titles and platforms, supporting durable revenue streams if engagement is maintained.

Consistent positive operating and free cash flow, including a 2025 rebound, underpin financial resilience. Reliable cash generation funds live-ops, user acquisition, and product investment without heavy borrowing, supporting sustained operations and strategic flexibility over the medium term.

Margins improved materially versus prior loss years, reflecting tighter marketing and better in-app monetization. Higher gross and net margins indicate a structurally more profitable cost base and greater ability to convert bookings into sustainable earnings over coming quarters.

bear.b: Multi-year revenue contraction signals weakening scale and user-base monetization breadth. Even with margin gains, declining top line reduces absolute cash and operating leverage, making long-term investment in new titles and UA more difficult without reversing user or bookings trends.

Negative equity is a structural balance-sheet weakness that limits financial flexibility and increases insolvency risk in stress scenarios. It constrains options for equity financing and can elevate scrutiny from partners or lenders during industry downturns or big product investments.

bear.t: Volatile Cash Generation And Thin Operating Cash
bear.b: Quarterly operating cash flow volatility and recent low OCF despite profit improvements show cash conversion risk. Irregular cash flow hampers consistent reinvestment in live-ops, marketing, and new development, increasing execution risk over the medium term.
